title: ardisia guadeloupensis var. genuina
intro: Ardisia guadeloupensis var. genuina, also known as the Guadeloupe ardisia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae, native to the Caribbean island of Guadeloupe. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ardisia guadeloupensis var. genuina include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by drying the leaves and roots, then infusing them in hot water to create teas, or by using the plant's extracts in capsule or tincture form.
